Elite Learners is now part of the New York Social Care Network (SCN) program.

The SCN (Social Care Network) program is a New York State initiative that connects Medicaid members to support for health-related social needs, including housing assistance, meal delivery, grocery support, pantry stocking, and transportation services.

I would like to introduce you to Betty Nicholas, who leads the SCN program. Betty will be a great resource and can provide additional information and support. All referrals of your clients who are on Medicaid should be directed to Betty for evaluation and assessment. You may reach her using the contact information below:

* Betty Nicolas
* Email: bnicolas@elitelearners.org
* Phone: 917-560-8935
name: Elite Learners, Fuel the Love

description:
How to Register (New Registrants):
1) Visit ( market.metcouncil.org ) to begin. Click “Order Food” and choose “Sign Up”
2) Register yourself and your household members and wait to hear from your Pantry Manager
3) Once you hear from your Pantry Manager, log in at market.metcouncil.org and start ordering
4) Choose a pick-up time during Pantry hours
5) Check email for confirmation and your receipt
How to Place Your Order Using Market:
1) Visit market.metcouncil.org to begin. Log in with your email address or phone number. Use PASSWORD Market@9502
2) Start shopping for items
3) When ready to place an order, click the shopping cart icon
4) Choose a pick-up time during Pantry hours
5) Check email for confirmation and receipt
H.E.S. Pantry Guidelines: You can utilize our Food Pantry once a month. You must come at your scheduled appointment date and time.  Please understand that no boxes will be held past your appointment date.

On-site registration required

description_es:
Los invitados tienen la flexibilidad de realizar pedidos en persona y recibir comida en el momento u optar por la comodidad de realizar [pedidos en línea](https://market.metcouncil.org/s/login/SelfRegister?language=en_US.) por adelantado . Nuestro sistema digital asigna puntos según el tamaño de la familia, lo que permite a los huéspedes elegir entre los elementos del menú que se adaptan a sus preferencias.

Nuestro sistema digital requiere un número de teléfono y una dirección de correo electrónico válidos en EE. UU. Esta información es importante porque es la mejor manera de contactar a los invitados y también se utiliza como nombre de usuario único de los invitados para nuestro sistema.
description:
Guests have the flexibility to place orders in person and receive food on the spot or opt for the convenience of placing [orders online](https://market.metcouncil.org/s/login/SelfRegister?language=en_US.) in advance. Our digital system allocates points based on family size, allowing guests to choose from menu items that suits their preferences.

Our digital system does require a valid USA phone number and email address. This information is important because its the best way we contact guests and also its used as the guests unique username for our system.

description:
IN-OFFICE SERVICES
**AFFORDABLE HOUSING COUNSELING**
Every Tuesday, from 11AM to 4PM, our office hosts an experienced affordable housing counselor to help constituents with assistance on applying for the NYC Affordable Housing Lottery. This is provided in partnership with CUFFH.
**HOMEOWNERSHIP COUNSELING**
Every other Thursday, from 10AM to 3PM, our office hosts an experienced homeownership counselor to guide constituents with homeownership, foreclosure, and financing. This is provided in partnership with Neighborhood Housing Services of Bedford-Stuyvesant.
**IMMIGRATION LAW COUNSELING**
Every other Wednesday, from 10AM to 4PM, we have an experienced immigration lawyer to assist with citizenship applications, understanding your rights, and other immigration-related legal issues.
**CIVIL LAW COUNSELING**
Every other Thursday, from 10AM to 4PM, we have an experienced lawyer available to provide legal assistance on a wide range of issues, with a specialization in landlord and tenant matters.
**VETERANS BENEFITS ASSISTANCE**
On the 2nd Friday from 10AM to 5PM of every month, we host a veteran service officer who helps veterans and their families obtain health, dependent, education, home loan program, and other state and local benefits.

description:
One Community is working to create a sustainable program in pursuit of the goals of eliminating food insecurity in Fort Greene and Clinton Hill and improving the purchasing power of local low-income residents. The program supports participating families' budgets by providing a substantial and predictable amount of varied, nutritious, and appealing food every week.

Fresh Pantry Locations:

[The Ingersoll Fresh Pantry](https://www.foodhelpline.org/directory/resources/ckz7yydv9000204sbckg0uoo3) is run at the **Ingersoll Community Center** weekly on Saturdays from **11 AM - 1 PM**. The location is **177 Myrtle Avenue, Brooklyn, NY 11201**.

[The Farragut Pantry](https://www.foodhelpline.org/directory/resources/421) is run at the **Church of the Open Door** weekly on Saturdays from **10:30 AM – 12:30 PM**. The location is **201 Gold St, Brooklyn, NY 11201**.
name: One Community

description:
To register, visit St. John’s Bread & Life (in person) Monday through Thursday at 795 Lexington Avenue in Brooklyn from 8:00am – 1:00pm. The pantry is open till 2:00pm but you will want to come earlier to give us time to register you and set you up with a Bread & Life shopping ID card.
Bring at least one proof of ID and one proof of address. See below for examples.
Examples of Photo ID
Driver License/Non-Driver ID
Passport Green Card/Work Permit
Employee/Student ID
Examples of Proof of Address
Gas, Electric, Cable, Telephone, or Medical Bill
Shelter or other housing program letter
Voter Registration Card
Lease/ Rent receipt
Social Security letter
Tax Return
Pay Stub
Bank StatementArrive early to receive a number. Diapers for the children under 2.
Hot meals and help with housing and EBT services may be available.
name: St. John's Bread and Life

description:
https://www.trinityhumanservices.org/programs/emergency-food-pantry

Our Client Choice Food Pantry is a modern, supermarket-style pantry where customers can shop for and choose the food they want. They can also reserve a time to visit the Food Pantry by using the mobile app Plentiful that allows customers to make appointments to shop. From July-October, our Food Pantry features fresh organic fruits and vegetables grown at local farms through United Way’s Local Produce Link program.


Our goal is to eliminate food insecurity by connecting clients to food and to resources and education they need to help lift them and their families out of crisis.


We are seeking volunteers  to help at the pantry. THSC Food Pantry is the only emergency food program in the area that is open four days a week, Tuesday – Friday, 9 am – 12 pm and 1– 4 pm.


How to make an appointment: 

A) Text "TRINITYHSC" to PANTRY (726-879) to make a reservation.

B) Apply online at http://www.plentifulapp.com

C) Call us at (718) 388-3176

D) Register on site in person with photo ID

description:
come at 3:30 to be one of the first on line bring a valid ID proof of address you can find us at 141 Manhattan Ave. 11206 we’re a small church in the middle of the block on Thursdays. People start lining up between three and 330 Madeline she’s always there. She’s the one in charge of taking in your information. She will give you all the information that you need once you give your ID she’ll make you should make your ticket there. All you have to do is just hand the ticket. She’ll scan it and she’ll find all your information that you have given to her prior to your registration. Everything is done by computer now so at the first time it might take a few minutes for you to register, but once you registered second time, if you show your ticket, she scans it walk right in take a seat and wait for the numbers to be called. Once your number is called you come and you get your food and you’re on your way. I hope this information that I have given you will be very helpful. I am behind the scenes. I do all the computer work in the back. You barely see me but if this information, I hope it has been helpful. Have a nice day and thank you very much. The Food Pantry schedule starts from 430 to 6 630 I believe sometimes if we have a couple of back left over and might run to seven thank you very much. Have a nice day.

On-site registration required
name: Valle De Jesus Servicing With Love
